notes: Flagship Alpha SMP server; 12-CPU configuration; Alpha 5/300 or 5/440 processor. Succeeded by Compaq AlphaServer GS series; discontinued after HP acquisition.
Alpha processors cited: 5/300, 5/440, 5/466. Last Alpha processor (EV79) canceled after HP acquisition. Alpha IP sold to Intel 2001.
Crossbar-switch interconnect clustering using Memory Channel technology contributed by Encore Computer. Enabled low-overhead SMP cluster expansion. Discontinued with DEC/Alpha.
64-bit Unix for Alpha; Very Large Memory support. Continued as Tru64 UNIX under Compaq/HP. Support ended ~2012.
600 MHz Alpha workstation starting at $10,000; dual OS support (Unix + NT); PowerStorm graphics. Discontinued with DEC acquisition.
DEC's 2D/3D graphics accelerator; OpenGL support; PCI bus. Discontinued with DEC acquisition.
Leading-edge Win95/NT Fortran compiler for Alpha and Intel. Technology later evolved into Intel Visual Fortran (after DEC semiconductor acquisition).
Automatic parallelization preprocessor for SMP servers. Technology evolved into Intel KAP products.
Industry-standard library for message-passing parallel applications. Still foundational to HPC; MPI-4.0 standardized 2021.
High-level language for parallel cluster applications. Declined in favor of MPI/OpenMP; never widely adopted in production.
IBM's MPP architecture; Aberdeen criticized as overly expensive for typical HPTC. Evolved into IBM Blue Gene and Power Systems; defeated Kasparov in chess (Deep Blue).
MIPS-based Unix workstations. Aberdeen noted SGI's lack of NT transition commitment. SGI discontinued IRIX hardware; SGI went bankrupt 2009.
